title: Midnight Rain
excerpt:
The rain speaks in a language
only the lonely understand,
each drop a word unsaid,
each puddle a memory pooled.
body:
The rain speaks in a language
only the lonely understand,
each drop a word unsaid,
each puddle a memory pooled.

I stood at the window
watching the world dissolve,
wondering if the sky too
had someone it couldn't forget.

Midnight rain — you are
the only honest thing
in a world that learned
to smile through its storms.

title: Letters Never Sent
excerpt:
I wrote you a hundred letters
in the margins of my sleep,
words that tasted like goodbye
but felt like please stay.
body:
I wrote you a hundred letters
in the margins of my sleep,
words that tasted like goodbye
but felt like please stay.

Each one folded into silence,
tucked beneath the weight
of everything I almost said
when you were still within reach.

Maybe one day the wind
will carry them to you —
and you'll know, without opening them,
that they were always yours.

title: The Space Between Stars
excerpt:
We are not the stars, my love,
we are the space between them —
the dark that makes the light
worth looking for.
body:
We are not the stars, my love,
we are the space between them —
the dark that makes the light
worth looking for.

In the vast quiet of the universe
I found you, not shining,
but present — the way silence
is present after a song ends.

And I thought: this is enough.
This is more than enough.
To be the space where someone
chooses to rest their eyes.

title: Autumn Knows
excerpt:
Autumn knows how to let go
without making it look like loss —
it turns everything golden first,
then releases.
body:
Autumn knows how to let go
without making it look like loss —
it turns everything golden first,
then releases.

I am learning from the trees.
How to shed what no longer serves,
how to stand bare and unashamed
and trust that spring remembers.

Every leaf that falls
is a lesson in surrender —
not defeat, but grace.
Not ending, but becoming.

title: Soft Grief
excerpt:
There is a grief that doesn't scream.
It sits with you at breakfast,
stirs your coffee slowly,
and says nothing.
body:
There is a grief that doesn't scream.
It sits with you at breakfast,
stirs your coffee slowly,
and says nothing.

It follows you to work,
waits outside the meeting room,
walks home beside you
when the streetlights come on.

Soft grief is the hardest kind —
because no one sees it,
and you learn to carry it
like a second heartbeat.

title: Still Here
excerpt:
After everything —
the storms, the silences,
the nights that had no morning —
I am still here.
body:
After everything —
the storms, the silences,
the nights that had no morning —
I am still here.

Not unbroken. Not the same.
But here, with coffee in my hands
and sunlight on the floor
and something that feels like hope.

Still here is enough.
Still here is everything.
Still here is the bravest thing
I have ever done.
